title: Influence of political events on ideology
description: Test your skills on how political events influence political attitudes!
topic_path: Influence of political events on ideology | 4.4
Influence of political events on ideology

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f7bb31eb6ec9dbe50063b26fe8e4c07d.svg)

>-Source: [Pew Research Center](https://www.people-press.org/2019/04/11/public-trust-in-government-1958-2019/)

**Based on the information in the line graph, which of the following is the most likely implication of the data in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Public attitudes on whether the government can solve social and economic problems are more likely to decline as people have less trust in government.
People are more likely to vote for Democratic candidates, as people have less trust in the government.
People are less likely to support third-party candidates for president, as they have less trust in the government.
Public attitudes on whether their vote matters often stay the same despite changes in how many people trust the government.

assessment_items.question_content:
>“9/11 significantly increased political participation of the affected populations . . . Families and neighbors became more involved in politicized activities, including the selection of party nominees and in providing financial support to favored candidates . . . Finally, although families, and to a lesser extent neighbors, became more involved in their own parties’ activities, there was an average shift in allegiance to the Republican Party.”

>-Source: Eitan D. Hersh, “[Long-term effect of September 11 on the political behavior of victims’ families and neighbors](http://www.pnas.org/content/110/52/20959),” 2013

**Which of the following statements is most consistent with the author’s argument in this passage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Cultural factors, like major international incidents, can affect people’s political attitudes.
People are more likely to be politically engaged when their parents are politically engaged.
Socioeconomic factors, like annual income, can affect which party people support.
People are more likely to support Republican candidates for the presidency when the United States is at war.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6d6f98cbb8d71c190e44c7fec1364615.svg)

>-Source: Gallup, [link to data](https://news.gallup.com/poll/1642/russia.aspx)

**Which of the following statements is reflected in the data in the line graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Major political events can cause shifts in public opinion.
Political attitudes rarely shift as a person ages.
Generational divides can impact a person’s political attitudes.
Politically engaged people are more likely to vote in elections.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6d6f98cbb8d71c190e44c7fec1364615.svg)

>-Source: Gallup, [link to data](https://news.gallup.com/poll/1642/russia.aspx)

**Which of the following statements best represents a potential reason for the change in public opinion toward Russia in 2015?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The FBI released a report that revealed that Russia started several cyberattacks on the United States, a major political event.
The number of 18- to 24-year-olds voting in elections increased to its highest level in fifty years, a major change in voter turnout.
The Republican Party won the presidency and the majority in the House of Representatives and the Senate, a major party realignment.
The US economy struggled after a recession, a major economic issue.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Graph 1:**
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dc3edfe75c4a7750b79e7fb59eff020b.gif)

**Graph 2:**
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7545a81d73c492991f5a7e8a71e63af8.gif)

>-Source: Pew Research Center, “Public Attitudes Toward the War in Iraq: 2003-2008,” [link to report,](https://www.pewresearch.org/2008/03/19/public-attitudes-toward-the-war-in-iraq-20032008/) 2008

**Which of the following best describes a trend in the graphs above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: As the Iraq War progressed, more Americans viewed the war itself and the decision to enter the war negatively.
As the Iraq War progressed, more Americans supported the decision to enter the war as they felt it was going well.
As the Iraq War progressed, most Americans supported spending more money on the military to end the war quickly.
As the Iraq War progressed, more Americans reported trusting the decisions of the federal government.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Graph 1:**
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dc3edfe75c4a7750b79e7fb59eff020b.gif)

**Graph 2:**
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7545a81d73c492991f5a7e8a71e63af8.gif)

>-Source: Pew Research Center, “Public Attitudes Toward the War in Iraq: 2003-2008,” [link to report,](https://www.pewresearch.org/2008/03/19/public-attitudes-toward-the-war-in-iraq-20032008/) 2008

**Based on the information in the two graphs, which of the following is the most likely implication of political events on political attitudes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: When the United States engages in long-lasting foreign conflicts, public attitudes toward international events become more unfavorable over time.
When the United States engages in short-term foreign conflicts, public attitudes towards international events stay relatively the same over time.
As people age, they are more likely to support US involvement in international affairs and long-term military engagements.
As people age, they are more likely to support efforts that keep the United States out of international conflicts.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Graph 1:**
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dc3edfe75c4a7750b79e7fb59eff020b.gif)

**Graph 2:**
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7545a81d73c492991f5a7e8a71e63af8.gif)

>-Source: Pew Research Center, “Public Attitudes Toward the War in Iraq: 2003-2008,” [link to report,](https://www.pewresearch.org/2008/03/19/public-attitudes-toward-the-war-in-iraq-20032008/) 2008

**Which of the following is an accurate conclusion based on a comparison of the trends in the line graphs and your knowledge of political attitudes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Major political events, like wars, can change political attitudes in the American public.
People’s attitudes about international conflicts depend on whether the United States wins.
Political attitudes rarely change, even when there are major political events like international conflicts.
Generational divides are the strongest indicator for a person’s political attitudes.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a6f6a6527574752e75391f50b2936664.png)

>-Source: Our World In Data, [link to report](https://ourworldindata.org/terrorism#public-opinion-on-terrorism)

**Which of the following best describes a trend in the line graph above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Major terrorist attacks shifted political attitudes about the threat of terrorism.
As more terrorist attacks occurred, fears about the threat of terrorism reached an all-time high.
Although there have been more terrorist attacks in the last twenty years, political attitudes about terrorism have remained relatively the same over time.
The youngest generation of voters is more concerned about terrorist attacks than the oldest generation.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f7bb31eb6ec9dbe50063b26fe8e4c07d.svg)

>-Source: [Pew Research Center](https://www.people-press.org/2019/04/11/public-trust-in-government-1958-2019/)

**Which of the following accurately describes the information presented in the line graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The percentage of Americans who said they trust the government fell significantly between the late 1960s and 1980, and since then has never risen to pre-1970 levels.
The percentage of Americans who said they trust the government fell significantly between the late 1960s and 1980, but since then has recovered to pre-1970 levels.
The percentage of Americans who said they trust the government has fallen over the course of every president’s term in office.
The percentage of Americans who said they trust the government was lowest during Nixon’s presidency.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f7bb31eb6ec9dbe50063b26fe8e4c07d.svg)

>-Source: [Pew Research Center](https://www.people-press.org/2019/04/11/public-trust-in-government-1958-2019/)


**The data displayed in the graph above best supports which of the following statements?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The war in Vietnam and the Watergate scandal, which occurred during the late 1960s and 1970s, eroded public trust in government that has never been fully rebuilt.
Ronald Reagan, who won landslide victories in the elections of 1980 and 1984, enjoyed growing public support for the government throughout his presidency.
The terrorist attacks of September 11, 2001 had an immediate negative effect on the level of trust Americans felt for the government.
Barack Obama’s historic victory in the election of 2008 led Americans to feel higher levels of trust in government than they did during George W. Bush’s administration.

assessment_items.question_content:
>“The effects of a severe recession experienced are large when the individual is between the ages of 18 and 24 – the so-called formative age – during which social psychologists think most of social beliefs are formed; the effects are not so strong when the recession is experienced later in life . . . these effects are permanent because attitudes of recession-stricken individuals remain significantly altered many years after the severe recession ends.”

>-Source: Antonio Spilimbergo and Paola Giuliano, “[The long-lasting effects of the economic crisis](https://voxeu.org/article/long-lasting-socio-political-effects-economic-crisis),” 2009

**Which of the following statements is most consistent with the authors’ argument in this passage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Economic recessions have lasting consequences on the attitudes of individuals who experienced them during their formative years.
Economic recessions have little impact on the attitudes of individuals who were not in their formative years when they occurred.
Social beliefs are formed during the ages of 18 and 24.
Recessions that strike when individuals are aged 18 to 24 cause them to experience lasting economic problems.

assessment_items.question_content:
>“9/11 significantly increased political participation of the affected populations . . . Families and neighbors became more involved in politicized activities, including the selection of party nominees and in providing financial support to favored candidates . . . Finally, although families, and to a lesser extent neighbors, became more involved in their own parties’ activities, there was an average shift in allegiance to the Republican Party.”

>-Source:  Eitan D. Hersh, “[Long-term effect of September 11 on the political behavior of victims’ families and neighbors](http://www.pnas.org/content/110/52/20959),” 2013

**Based on the text, which of the following statements would the author most likely agree with?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Major political events play a significant role in shaping individuals' political attitudes.
Neighbors of 9/11 victims became more politically active than family members of victims.
Traumatic events tend to sway political attitudes towards the Republican Party.
Financial support for candidates is not a meaningful form of political participation.
